hero.headline: State handling is part of the product proof, not hidden glue.
hero.summary: The repo makes plugin source-path drift, hook locality, and release-gating evidence visible through checked-in docs plus scored benchmark outputs.

sections.title: State-management visibility comes first
sections.summary: The repo treats state as a first-class contract: canonical plugin source path, explicit user-global versus project-local split, and dedicated validation commands.
display_points: Canonical plugin source path stays rooted at packages/copilot-cli-plugin/ rather than transient .omx/team worktrees.
Project-local hook evidence remains under .copilot-hooks/ while user-global Copilot state remains under ~/.copilot/.
State drift is release-relevant and called out separately from benchmark scoring.

sections.title: Authenticated flows and fallback paths are explicit
sections.summary: Bootstrap and live prompt-smoke paths require authenticated Copilot tooling, but low-friction validators still work before install/auth succeeds.
display_points: Bootstrap expects both copilot and gh on PATH and authenticated for the environment.
Live prompt smoke depends on a signed-in Copilot CLI session with model access.
Low-friction validation still starts with docs, root-surface, power-surface, and state-contract checks.

sections.title: Cursor remains a comparison input, not claimed support
sections.summary: Cursor sources are retained for sibling planning and cross-host comparison only. The repo keeps that boundary visible instead of presenting Cursor as a validated host.
display_points: Cursor docs inform comparison and sibling oh-my-cursor planning only.
Release and limitation docs explicitly reject treating Cursor language as shipping proof.
Any future Cursor support would require a new approved plan and direct proof.

scoreboard.callout: Current checked-in enhanced quick and full evaluations both pass at 100/100.
